refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is already focused on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is solution-neutral as it does not specify how to achieve this, only what is required. Therefore, it is atomic and does not need further decomposition.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits
Use error detection and correction mechanisms to recover lost packets and ensure communication quality
Design redundant communication paths to provide failover and reduce effective packet loss
Incorporate real-time network quality monitoring and feedback to teleoperation control system
Apply data compression and prioritization to optimize bandwidth usage and reduce packet loss
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol adjustments and teleoperation control timing
Dependency on network quality monitoring data
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical solution concept of adaptive communication control, which can be modularized independently from other system functions. Potential coupling arises if network monitoring tightly integrates with teleoperation control, but this can be managed by clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions can be monitored accurately in real-time
Risk that adaptive protocol complexity may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness
Assumes customer-defined packet loss limit is achievable under agreed network conditions
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining interfaces between communication adaptation logic and teleoperation control modules to ensure modularity and reduce coupling.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the network gateway and communication management subsystems responsible for handling communication protocols, network monitoring, and adaptive transmission control.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends monitored network performance data and status reports to other system modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interfaces with the physical and logical network layers to adjust transmission parameters and monitor packet loss.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the adaptive communication protocol logically belongs to the network management functions within NGHE Solution. Minimal diffusion as most related functions are internal.
Vehicle module primarily handles physical vehicle control and sensors; adaptive communication protocol would require significant cross-module interaction, causing diffusion.
Teleoperation depends on communication but is more focused on control commands and user interface; embedding adaptive protocol here would cause coupling with network management.
Safety Services focus on monitoring and alerts; adaptive communication protocol is not central and would cause high diffusion if placed here.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the adaptive communication protocol DP because it directly manages network communication and can encapsulate the monitoring and adjustment logic with minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules may require careful interface design to avoid coupling.
Changes in network hardware or protocols might require NGHE Solution to interface with other modules, increasing diffusion risk.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by implementing an adaptive communication protocol that monitors and adjusts transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within the customer-defined limit, fulfilling the functional requirement for teleoperation communication quality.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules may require careful interface design to avoid coupling.
Changes in network hardware or protocols might require NGHE Solution to interface with other modules, increasing diffusion risk.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is highly cohesive for containing the adaptive communication protocol DP, as it encompasses network gateway and communication management subsystems. The module containment score is high (9) with minimal diffusion (2), indicating good encapsulation of related functions.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface and ensuring packet loss remains within the ≤1% acceptance criterion under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and appropriate, as this module owns the network communication functions necessary to realize the FR.

recommended_revisions: Design interfaces carefully to minimize coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, especially for real-time data dependencies.
Plan for modular updates to accommodate changes in network hardware or protocols to reduce diffusion risk.
Include explicit verification steps in the Telemetry interface to monitor packet loss and confirm compliance with acceptance criteria.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear verification feasibility, appropriate allocation, and high module containment scores. The adaptive communication protocol DP directly addresses the FR, and the NGHE Solution IM is well-suited to contain the DP with minimal diffusion risk. Although there are some diffusion risks related to dependencies and hardware changes, these are manageable through careful interface design and modular updates as recommended. Given the high safety criticality, the current level is sufficiently detailed and verified to proceed without further sub-FR decomposition.

revise.why: Incorporate recommended interface design improvements and modular update plans to mitigate diffusion risks and ensure verification steps for packet loss monitoring are explicitly included.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can effectively encapsulate the adaptive communication protocol.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules can be designed to minimize coupling.
Verification through telemetry monitoring is reliable under agreed network conditions.
open_risks: Potential coupling due to real-time data dependencies.
Impact of network hardware or protocol changes on module interfaces.
evidence_needed: Verification data confirming packet loss remains ≤1% under operational conditions.
Interface design documentation demonstrating minimized coupling and modular update capability.
